title: Connect and Operate MCP in Codex and Claude · Spiral Review
summary: Configure one safe server in both Codex and Claude Code. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure scoped Codex STDIO or HTTP servers, timeouts, tools, approvals, and authentication.
Configure Claude Code local, project, or user MCP scope and review shared server trust.
Verify initialization, primitive discovery, tool calls, errors, and shutdown in each client.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Codex Config · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext clients. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Configure scoped Codex STDIO or HTTP servers, timeouts, tools, approvals, and authentication. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext clients. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Codex Config, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests.

content.text: ChatGPT web reads local Codex MCP configuration. This fails the Codex Config contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext clients. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

stimulus.title: Support Runbook case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext clients. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Reinstalling the server is the first fix for every issue.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a connection, auth, timeout, output-limit, and version troubleshooting runbook. Artifact under review: cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Support Runbook, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Support Runbook applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a connection, auth, timeout, output-limit, and version troubleshooting runbook.
